items.title: Shuffle every epoch
items.avoid: Iterating batches in fixed file or time order across epochs.
items.prefer: Random permutation of indices each epoch via `shuffle=True` or manual `np.random.permutation`.
items.why: Random batches approximate the full data distribution and prevent correlated gradient bias.
items.example: DataLoader(train_set, batch_size=64, shuffle=True) instead of shuffle=False on sorted CSV rows.

items.title: Match batch size to hardware and memory
items.avoid: Setting batch size so large that GPU runs out of memory or so small that matrix ops underutilize the device.
items.prefer: Start with a power-of-two batch (32–256), increase until memory is ~80% used, then tune learning rate if batch size changes.
items.why: Batch size affects noise, throughput, and often the optimal learning rate (linear scaling rule is a common starting heuristic).
items.example: If doubling batch from 64→128 fits in VRAM, try doubling lr from 0.01→0.02 and compare validation curves.

items.title: Separate training noise from validation signal
items.avoid: Stopping or checkpointing based on a single batch loss spike.
items.prefer: Track epoch-level training loss average and validation loss; use early stopping on validation.
items.why: SGD noise is expected; validation on held-out data measures generalization.
items.example: Save best checkpoint when `val_loss` hits a new minimum, not when `train_batch_loss` dips on one iteration.
